expectations.text: Identifies 'assignees.count' assertion as redundant / owned by the Task model spec
expectations.evidence: Says 'Restructure: use change { }.by(1) instead' — gives wrong advice (improve the form) instead of identifying cross-ownership and deleting it

expectations.text: Identifies 'assignee.full_name' assertion as redundant / owned by the User model spec
expectations.evidence: Identifies it as problematic ('expected value derived from same object') though frames it as implementation-derived value rather than cross-ownership

expectations.text: Identifies 'respond_to(:full_name)' as tautological (tests presence not behavior)
expectations.evidence: 'Remove: tests existence, not behavior; zero coverage value'

expectations.text: Correctly identifies the two GOOD tests as keepers ('updates the assignee', 'enqueues the notification job')
expectations.evidence: Labels both as 'Keep' in the summary table

expectations.text: Explains the receiver-owns-assertion principle (not just says 'delete it')
expectations.evidence: Uses 'derived expected value' and 'weak form' framing. Does not mention receiver-owns principle, message direction, or cross-ownership.
